'''James Meadows''' (born 15 May 1999) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so|sh|hk}} or {{rlp|fb}} for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and spent time on loan from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[London Skolars]] and the [[Coventry Bears]] in [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]], and the [[Sheffield Eagles]] in the [[2019 Betfred Championship|Betfred Championship]]. Meadows has also played for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] and the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Career==
===London Broncos===
Meadows made his professional début for the [[London Broncos]] against the [[Barrow Raiders]] on 4 February 2018 in round 1 of the [[Betfred Championship|Championship]].

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 12 October 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Oct 2021|accessdate=12 Oct 2021|title=London Star James Meadows makes move north ahead of 2022|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-star-james-meadows-makes-move-north-ahead-of-2022/}}</ref>

He is now head coach of University of Leeds Rugby League team alongside his team mates Aidan McGowan (Backs and fighting coach) and [[Josh Hodson]] (Middles and Wrestling coach).

===London Broncos (re-join)===
On 10 November 2023 it was reported that he had re-signed for the London Broncos in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=10 Nov 2023|title=London Broncos' signing spree continues with third of the day as Championship playmaker returns to the capital|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-broncos-signing-spree-continues-with-third-of-the-day-as-championship-playmaker-returns-to-the-capital}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 3 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Yorkshire Live|date=3 Jul 2024|accessdate=10 Oct 2024|author=Matthew Shaw|title=Bradford Bulls sign Super League halfback as recruitment frenzy continues|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/bradford-bulls-sign-super-league-29463710}}</ref>

===London Broncos (re-join)===
On 4 December 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=4 Dec 2025|author=Callum Walker|title=London Broncos snap up released Bradford Bulls halfback|url=https://www.totalrl.com/london-broncos-snap-up-released-bradford-bulls-halfback/}}</ref>

In February 2026 Meadows set a new club record of 42 points in a game. He scored two tries and converted 17 out of 18 attempts at goal in London's win over the [[North Wales Crusaders]] at [[Plough Lane]].<ref>[https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/london-broncos-set-incredible-record-as-png-star-scores-hat-trick-in-18-try-win-113147/amp/ London Broncos set incredible record as PNG star scores hat-trick in 18-try win]</ref>
